# 11 Hybrid Event Examples to Inspire Your Next Strategy

![Nina Lelidou](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpostauthor%2Fclyd4c6c2001h5zne5vxxs9o5%2F1720451392369-536372774.jpeg&w=96&q=75)

Nina Lelidou

Content Contributor, HeySummit

Hybrid events have taken the spotlight in recent years, and it’s no wonder why. But let’s face it - planning one can be a real headache!

Luckily, you don’t have to start from scratch.

Many event planners have created amazing hybrid events by putting their own spin on the format. That's why **we've pulled together 11** [hybrid event](https://heysummit.com/solutions/hybrid-events) **examples that saw a lot of success**, so you can gather some inspiration for *your* next [hybrid event](https://heysummit.com/solutions/hybrid-events).

Whether you’re looking for fresh ideas to connect with your audience, ways to make virtual attendees feel just as involved, or smart tips on navigating the hybrid setup, these strategies might be just what you need to bring new life into your own planning process.

## How Does a Hybrid Event Work?

**![Hybrid events](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpost%2Fundefined%2F1729937069915-772400095.png&w=3840&q=75)**

![Hybrid events](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpost%2Fundefined%2F1729937069915-772400095.png&w=3840&q=75)

In a hybrid event, you’re essentially creating two experiences at once: the in-person and the virtual.

Both groups are connected by a shared program and purpose so they can participate in things like live Q&As, [interactive sessions](/event-terms/interactive-sessions), and networking breaks. The idea is to make sure both the in-person and virtual audiences can engage and get value from the event, even if they’re experiencing it differently.

### Types of hybrid events: internal vs. external

Not all hybrid events are created equal, and knowing the difference between internal and external events can help you pick the right one for your goals.

**Internal hybrid events**: Think corporate training sessions or company-wide meetings where the audience is limited to employees or close partners. The focus here is on secure access, simple tools and often more emphasis on interaction. These are all about team bonding and sharing knowledge.

**External hybrid events**: These are the public-facing events, like product launches, conferences, or webinars, where you’re reaching a broader audience. Here the focus is on accessibility, scalability, and sometimes even entertainment value to keep people engaged. The goal is to increase brand awareness and create memorable experiences.

***Why choose one over the other?*** It’s all about what you’re hoping to achieve. Internal events focus on creating an interactive, team-centered experience, while external events often prioritize reach and brand engagement.

**🔗 Related:** [Learn How to Plan the Perfect Hybrid Event](https://heysummit.com/blog/how-to-plan-a-hybrid-event)

## Benefits of Hybrid Events

**![The benefits of hybrid events](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpost%2Fundefined%2F1729937069714-985320194.png&w=3840&q=75)**

![The benefits of hybrid events](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpost%2Fundefined%2F1729937069714-985320194.png&w=3840&q=75)

Here are a few ways hybrid events stand out:

### They are flexible

Hybrid events let you adapt to changing needs with ease. If some speakers can’t make it in person or your audience numbers shift, no problem - you have a virtual option to lean on!

This setup is all about having options. You can scale up or down and accommodate last-minute changes without breaking a sweat.

### They give your brand a lot of visibility

Running a hybrid event isn’t just about the event day itself. With recordings, on-demand sessions, and the buzz created by social sharing, your event can live on well past its original date.

This extended lifespan gives your brand more visibility, allowing it to reach audiences you wouldn’t normally have access to.

### You might get more sponsorship opportunities

Sponsors love hybrid events because they can get visibility with both in-person and online attendees; doubling their exposure.

From branded virtual backgrounds to signage opportunities in [in-person events](https://heysummit.com/solutions/in-person-events), there are tons of ways to integrate sponsors in both settings and add a valuable revenue stream to your event!

### They offer diverse engagement options

Hybrid events allow you to mix and match engagement tools that work for both in-person and virtual event attendees.

For example, you can use interactive Q&As and [live polling](/event-terms/live-polling) for remote participants, and hands-on activities or [breakout sessions](/event-terms/breakout-sessions) for the in-person audiences. This variety helps you keep everyone engaged, no matter where they are.

## What Makes a Successful Hybrid Event?

Pulling off a successful hybrid event means that you need to keep both in-person and virtual attendees engaged and connected without favoring one over the other.

*So, what separates the okay events from the ones everyone remembers?*

Here’s what really counts:

### Seamless tech setup

Nothing can ruin an event faster than tech issues. Event technology like a reliable platform for streaming, a good internet connection, and smooth integration between virtual and in-person elements are key. Test everything beforehand: run-throughs are your best friend!

### Engagement for both sides

A successful hybrid event makes sure both audiences feel included. Use polls, Q&A sessions, and interactive features for your virtual audience, and offer live networking or exclusive sessions for [in-person event](https://heysummit.com/solutions/in-person-events) participants. It’s all about making everyone feel like they’re part of the same experience.

### Clear communication

Hybrid events come with a lot of moving parts, so keeping everyone informed is essential. Send detailed schedules, login links, and venue maps ahead of time, so attendees (whether on-site or online) know exactly where to go and what to expect.

### Great content that speaks to all

Content is king - whether it’s for a live audience or people tuning in from home.

Successful hybrid events deliver sessions that are interesting and valuable for both groups. If you’re hosting speakers, make sure they’re engaging - whether they're on stage or on screen - and keep session lengths manageable to avoid virtual fatigue.

### Dedicated support teams

Having a support team for both in-person and virtual attendees makes a huge difference.

For in-person attendees, you need people on the ground to handle questions or direct traffic. Virtual attendees need a tech support team ready to jump in if anyone has trouble joining or streaming. It’s like having two hosts, each focused on making the experience smooth and stress-free for all.

### Post-event follow-up

The event doesn’t end when the last session wraps up!

Keep the momentum going with a follow-up email, a recording of sessions, or a highlight reel for those who couldn’t attend everything live. It’s a small touch that helps people feel like they got the full experience and keeps your brand top of mind.

## 11 Hybrid Event Examples (+ How They Stand Out!)

When it comes to hybrid events, creativity and strategy make all the difference.

Below you will find some of the best hybrid events examples that did more than just simply connect in-person and virtual audiences. They created unique, memorable experiences that kept attendees engaged long after the event was over!

### 1. Apple Product Launch

**![Apple hybrid event examples](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpost%2Fundefined%2F1729937069765-310646601.png&w=3840&q=75)**

![Apple hybrid event examples](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpost%2Fundefined%2F1729937069765-310646601.png&w=3840&q=75)

Apple's September 2024 product launch is a great example of hybrid events. It introduced the iPhone 16 Pro and Pro Max, Apple Watch Series 10, and AirPods 4, among other announcements.

This event featured a hybrid format, and offered a live stream to a global audience and in-person attendees at the Steve Jobs Theater.

**Impact**: By blending real-time virtual demos with live sessions, Apple maintained a strong, immersive experience that reached millions. This hybrid event format strengthened their reputation for top-tier hybrid experiences, with high engagement levels across platforms.

**How it stands out:**

**Immersive product use-cases:** Apple demonstrated real-life applications for products, using relatable, storytelling-based scenarios that resonated deeply with viewers​

**Interactive audience polls:** Live polls for virtual attendees to participate in real-time added an extra layer of engagement.

**Consistent branding:** Apple kept its branding sharp, from stage design to social media follow-ups, reinforcing brand loyalty throughout the entire event cycle.

### 2. Paris Fashion Week

**![Paris Fashion Week hybrid event](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpost%2Fundefined%2F1729937069876-657640816.png&w=3840&q=75)**

![Paris Fashion Week hybrid event](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpost%2Fundefined%2F1729937069876-657640816.png&w=3840&q=75)

Known for its exclusive appeal, Paris Fashion Week went hybrid, giving virtual attendees behind-the-scenes access, interviews with designers, and live Q&A during runway shows.

**Impact**: This dual format brought in a new, wider audience beyond the fashion elite. By making the event inclusive they expanded their brand reach and online engagement, especially through social media interactions.

**How it stands out:**

**Exclusive digital content:** Virtual viewers had access to backstage footage and Q&A sessions not available to in-person attendees​.

**Audience voting:** Fashion fans could vote on their favorite designs, bridging the gap between virtual and in-person experiences.

**Global reach:** The digital content made the event accessible to international fans who would otherwise miss it, turning the week into a global experience.

### 3. INBOUND by HubSpot

**![INBOUND hybrid event example](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpost%2Fundefined%2F1729937069773-504856014.png&w=3840&q=75)**

![INBOUND hybrid event example](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpost%2Fundefined%2F1729937069773-504856014.png&w=3840&q=75)

INBOUND 2024 combined on-site and online engagement with AI-driven matchmaking, helping both in-person and remote attendees build connections based on shared interests and event goals.

**Impact**: By matching participants based on shared interests and goals, INBOUND enabled more meaningful connections.

**How it stands out:**

**AI matchmaking tool:** Attendees could fill out detailed profiles in advance, and the AI tool suggested personalized connections for more meaningful networking​.

**Cross-platform networking:** Virtual and in-person participants could connect via scheduled digital meet-ups, reinforcing the hybrid event’s inclusivity.

**Speaker access:** The event app allowed participants to book follow-up meetings with speakers, making speaker access available to everyone.

### 4. Tomorrowland

**![Tomorrowland hybrid event example](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpost%2Fundefined%2F1729937069869-169724929.png&w=3840&q=75)**

![Tomorrowland hybrid event example](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpost%2Fundefined%2F1729937069869-169724929.png&w=3840&q=75)

Tomorrowland embraced a hybrid format in 2024 to offer both live and virtual experiences.

The event brought together an in-person audience of thousands, while fans worldwide could join through the "Tomorrowland Around the World" digital platform, which featured live performances, exclusive artist interviews, and interactive activities.

**Impact**: By integrating live-streamed performances, interactive stages, and virtual meet-and-greets, Tomorrowland created an engaging festival experience accessible from anywhere.

**How it stands out:**

**3D virtual stages**: Tomorrowland created digital replicas of their iconic stages, such as the Main Stage and Freedom Stage, allowing virtual attendees to immerse themselves in the festival’s signature visual atmosphere.

**Interactive festival map**: Virtual participants could explore different areas of the festival, interact with other virtual festivalgoers, and discover pop-up events, simulating the experience of being on-site.

**Live polls and** [**chat features**](/event-terms/chat-features): Virtual attendees could participate in live polls, engage in real-time chats with other fans, and join in Q&A sessions with performing artists, making the digital experience more interactive.

### 5. VivaTech

**![VivaTech hybrid event example](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpost%2Fundefined%2F1729937069886-298302985.png&w=3840&q=75)**

![VivaTech hybrid event example](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpost%2Fundefined%2F1729937069886-298302985.png&w=3840&q=75)

VivaTech hosted a hybrid experience in Paris, drawing tech enthusiasts and innovators from around the world. In-person attendees could explore interactive tech exhibitions and network directly, while virtual attendees accessed live-streamed talks, demos, and online networking sessions.

**Impact**: The hybrid model significantly expanded the event’s reach. The virtual component not only connected more people to the content but also fostered a global community of participants who engaged through social media and virtual discussions.

**How it stands out:**

**Seamless digital-physical integration**: VivaTech’s virtual experience allowed remote attendees to participate in real-time, with features like Q&A sessions, interactive live-streamed panels, and virtual expos that mirrored the on-ground excitement.

**Focus on global accessibility**: VivaTech’s hybrid format aimed to make high-quality tech content accessible globally, allowing anyone with internet access to learn about the latest innovations and participate in networking sessions.

**Engaging social media presence**: The event’s strong social media interaction included [real-time updates](/event-terms/real-time-updates), highlights, and live engagement with followers. This expanded the festival’s reach beyond traditional streaming.

### 6. Comic-Con

**![Comin-Con hybrid event example](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpost%2Fundefined%2F1729937069895-603472649.png&w=3840&q=75)**

![Comin-Con hybrid event example](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpost%2Fundefined%2F1729937069895-603472649.png&w=3840&q=75)

Comic-Con embraced a hybrid format by offering both live panels and on-demand recordings for global fans - giving access to Q&As, celebrity interviews, and exclusive sneak peeks.

**Impact**: This hybrid approach extended the event’s reach, attracting both traditional attendees and new online audiences who could enjoy Comic-Con from home.

**How it stands out:**

**On-demand access:** Attendees could access recorded sessions and rewatch highlights, increasing engagement beyond the live event.​

**Exclusive online content:** Virtual attendees enjoyed bonus content, such as pre-recorded panels and artist interviews.

**Interactive fan zones:** Online spaces for fans to discuss sessions in real-time helped create a lively, community-focused experience.

### 7. Sundance Film Festival

**![Sundadance example of hybrid events](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpost%2Fundefined%2F1729937069907-759401927.png&w=3840&q=75)**

![Sundadance example of hybrid events](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpost%2Fundefined%2F1729937069907-759401927.png&w=3840&q=75)

Sundance went hybrid with virtual screening rooms for remote audiences, complemented by interactive filmmaker Q&As.

**Impact**: This format allowed for greater accessibility. It expanded viewership to those unable to travel to Sundance, while still preserving the festival atmosphere.

**How it stands out:**

**Scheduled screenings:** Virtual screenings mirrored live ones, keeping remote audiences engaged on the same schedule.

**Personalized Q&A sessions:** Exclusive virtual Q&As allowed filmmakers to engage directly with audiences.

**Curated viewing options:** Remote attendees could build custom screening schedules for a tailored experience.

### 8. Microsoft Ignite

**![Microsoft Ignire example of hybrid events](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpost%2Fundefined%2F1729937069967-889165557.png&w=3840&q=75)**

![Microsoft Ignire example of hybrid events](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpost%2Fundefined%2F1729937069967-889165557.png&w=3840&q=75)

Microsoft Ignite hosts both in-person sessions and virtual demos. This allows for interactive, hands-on labs for remote attendees.

**Impact**: This model boosted global participation, especially among tech professionals who couldn’t attend in person, which also reinforced Microsoft’s commitment to accessibility.

**How it stands out:**

**Interactive labs:** Virtual attendees can join hands-on lab sessions.

**Access to product experts:** Both online and on-site attendees have opportunities for one-on-one discussions with Microsoft experts.

**Virtual certification exams:** Participants can earn certifications, which adds to the event’s value for professional development.

### 9. Adobe Summit

**![Adobe Summit hybrid event](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpost%2Fundefined%2F1729937069955-245980192.png&w=3840&q=75)**

![Adobe Summit hybrid event](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpost%2Fundefined%2F1729937069955-245980192.png&w=3840&q=75)

Adobe Summit, a premier event for digital marketing and customer experience provides both in-person and virtual participation, catering to a global audience.

**Impact**: The hybrid format allows Adobe to reach thousands of marketing professionals worldwide, providing access to industry-leading content, expert-led workshops, and interactive experiences.

**How it stands out:**

**Simultaneous demos:** Virtual and in-person attendees can join the same sessions for a unified experience.

**Digital product booths:** Remote booths with live reps allow for real-time Q&As.

**Hybrid networking lounges:** Virtual attendees can interact in themed [breakout rooms](/event-terms/breakout-rooms), mirroring the in-person lounge experience.

### 10. South by Southwest (SXSW)

**![SXSW hybrid event example](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpost%2Fundefined%2F1729937069934-576740811.png&w=3840&q=75)**

![SXSW hybrid event example](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpost%2Fundefined%2F1729937069934-576740811.png&w=3840&q=75)

SXSW blended live performances with virtual panels and sessions, letting international audiences engage with the festival’s creative events from anywhere.

**Impact**: The event boosted its global reach and attracted a larger audience, offering a fully hybrid experience that kept everyone engaged.

**How it stands out:**

**Global-friendly streaming:** Sessions were scheduled for multiple time zones, maximizing accessibility.

**Exclusive virtual panels:** These sessions allowed for deeper, focused discussions.

**Engaging digital content:** Virtual viewers enjoyed artist interviews, interactive panels, and exclusive performances​

### 11. EmTech Digital

**![Emtech Digital hybrid conference examples](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpost%2Fundefined%2F1729937069989-119699689.png&w=3840&q=75)**

![Emtech Digital hybrid conference examples](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fheysummit-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fpost%2Fundefined%2F1729937069989-119699689.png&w=3840&q=75)

EmTech Digital is a premier AI conference that offers a hybrid experience, allowing participants worldwide to explore the latest advancements in AI and machine learning.

**Impact**: The hybrid format expands EmTech’s reach, allowing global professionals to participate without needing to travel.

**How it stands out:**

**Real-time interaction**: Virtual attendees can engage directly with speakers through live Q&As, gaining insights alongside in-person participants.

**On-demand access**: All sessions are recorded, allowing attendees to revisit content post-event or catch up on missed sessions.

**Diverse networking opportunities**: The hybrid format includes virtual networking options, which lets online attendees connect with their peers globally.

## Best Practices for Marketing a Hybrid Event

### **Use a hybrid event platform**

Save time (and hassle) by using a hybrid event platform to automate parts of your event. HeySummit is an all-in-one [virtual event platform](https://heysummit.com) that manages registration, ticketing, and engagement for both in-person and virtual attendees from a single dashboard. This means that you can spend less time on logistics and more time connecting with your audience. Check out HeySummit's hybrid event solution [here](https://heysummit.com/solutions/hybrid-events).

### **Get the word out with a multi-channel strategy**

[Event marketing](https://heysummit.com/blog/event-marketing) is key if you want your hybrid event to be a hit. Start early by promoting it across channels that speak to both virtual and in-person audiences. Use social media, email, and your website to highlight key benefits - like exclusive networking for on-site guests and easy-access recordings for virtual participants.

### **Engage influencers and partners for added reach**

Collaborate with event industry influencers or affiliate partners to reach new audiences. Create promotional content that partners can share, like swipe-copy emails or branded graphics. You can even set up an affiliate program through HeySummit for partners to promote your event and earn commissions (which boosts both visibility and ticket sales).

### **Create exclusive content for in-person and digital audiences**

Make each group feel like they’re getting something unique by designing exclusive content for both virtual and on-site attendees. For example, you could offer in-person attendees [VIP access](/event-terms/vip-access) to behind-the-scenes tours, special networking sessions, or live workshops, while virtual attendees get exclusive interviews, Q&A sessions with speakers, or access to bonus digital content.

## How to Measure Hybrid Event Success

Here are some key metrics to track the success of your hybrid event:

**Attendance breakdown**: Compare the number of in-person and virtual attendees. A higher total count can indicate successful reach, especially if your virtual attendance brought in a significant boost.

**Engagement rates**: Track participation in activities like Q&As, polls, and session attendance. Look at metrics like the number of questions asked, virtual hand raises, or chat activity. High engagement means people were invested in the content!

**Session watch times**: For virtual sessions, measure how long people stayed logged in. High retention rates show that the content kept audiences hooked, while drop-off times can highlight areas needing improvement.

**Post-**[**event feedback**](/event-terms/event-feedback): Use surveys or quick feedback forms to gather opinions from both in-person and virtual attendees. Ask about session quality, ease of participation, and overall satisfaction to pinpoint what worked.

**Conversion metrics**: If the event was geared towards lead generation or sales, look at conversion data. Count leads captured, follow-ups completed, or sales made post-event to assess the ROI.

# Hybrid networking events

Hybrid networking events have reshaped how communities gather, connect, and collaborate. By blending physical and virtual participation, these events offer a dynamic solution for modern community building â one that accommodates diverse member needs, expands reach, and enhances inclusivity.

In a world where flexibility, accessibility, and engagement are paramount, hybrid networking events have moved from being a temporary response to global challenges to becoming a core strategic asset for forward-thinking communities.

## What are hybrid networking events?

Hybrid networking events are gatherings designed to seamlessly integrate both in-person and online attendees. Rather than treating virtual participation as an afterthought, successful hybrid events are intentionally structured to provide value, engagement, and networking opportunities to both audiences equally.

Key characteristics of hybrid networking events include:

A shared programme that allows live and remote attendees to interact with speakers, sessions, and each other

Technology integration such as live streaming, virtual breakout rooms, and interactive digital platforms

Flexibility for participants to choose how they attend based on preference, location, or circumstance

Opportunities for cross-channel engagement before, during, and after the event

The most effective hybrid events do not replicate the physical experience online. Instead, they craft complementary experiences that cater to the strengths and expectations of each audience type.

## Why hybrid networking events are essential for community building

Communities thrive on connection. Yet, barriers such as geography, cost, accessibility needs, and time constraints can prevent members from participating fully in physical events.

Hybrid networking events address these challenges by:

**Extending access:** Members who cannot travel still have meaningful ways to connect and contribute.

**Enhancing inclusivity:** Different participation formats accommodate varied needs, preferences, and abilities.

**Growing reach:** Events are no longer limited by venue size or location, allowing communities to engage a global audience.

**Fostering resilience:** Hybrid models make communities less vulnerable to external disruptions, from travel restrictions to unforeseen crises.

**Increasing return on investment:** Hybrid events can attract more participants, sponsors, and media attention, maximising the value of organisational resources.

In essence, hybrid networking events enable communities to remove barriers without sacrificing the intimacy and serendipity that make live interactions powerful.

## Core elements of a successful hybrid networking event

Designing a successful hybrid event requires more than simply adding a video feed to a physical meeting. It demands intentional planning across multiple dimensions.

### 1. Audience experience design

The goal is to create equitable and engaging experiences for both in-person and virtual participants. This includes:

Clear communication about how each experience will work

Tailored content tracks if necessary (e.g., exclusive online sessions or in-person meetups)

Interactive elements such as polls, Q&A, and live chat that bridge both audiences

Dedicated moderators or facilitators for each format

Treating virtual attendees as full participants â not passive viewers â is critical for engagement and satisfaction.

### 2. Technology infrastructure

Robust and reliable technology is the backbone of a hybrid event. Key considerations include:

High-quality video and audio streaming

Platforms that enable networking, breakout sessions, and content sharing

Backup systems to handle technical issues swiftly

User-friendly interfaces for both attendees and organisers

Investing in the right technology partners can make the difference between a seamless hybrid experience and a frustrating one.

### 3. Networking facilitation

Networking is often the most valued aspect of community events. Facilitating meaningful connections in a hybrid environment requires creativity:

Virtual networking lounges or speed networking sessions

Hybrid panels where speakers interact with both in-person and online audiences

Matchmaking algorithms to suggest connections based on interests or goals

Structured opportunities for virtual and physical participants to collaborate, such as joint projects or discussion groups

The objective is to design pathways that encourage serendipitous conversations, even across different mediums.

### 4. Content delivery strategy

Content must be adapted to suit both formats. This can include:

Shorter, punchier sessions to combat screen fatigue

Visual storytelling techniques that work well on camera

Recording sessions for on-demand viewing, extending the eventâs impact

Exclusive content drops for remote audiences to make virtual attendance feel rewarding

Balancing live engagement with asynchronous content access ensures that participation is flexible and rewarding.

### 5. Post-event engagement

Hybrid events should not end when the last session wraps up. Post-event strategies can maintain momentum and deepen community ties:

Sending personalised follow-ups to both audiences

Creating highlight reels and content recaps

Hosting post-event virtual meetups or Q&A sessions

Gathering feedback segmented by participation type to improve future events

A well-executed follow-up strategy turns a single event into a continuous engagement loop.

## Challenges and considerations

While hybrid networking events offer immense advantages, they also come with unique challenges that require careful planning:

**Cost:** Delivering a high-quality hybrid experience can be more expensive than a purely physical or virtual event.

**Complexity:** Managing two audience experiences simultaneously demands greater logistical coordination.

**Engagement disparity:** It can be easier for virtual participants to disengage if the event design does not actively include them.

**Technological failures:** Connectivity issues or platform glitches can disproportionately affect virtual attendeesâ experiences.

Successful hybrid events anticipate and plan for these risks, rather than reacting to them after they arise.

## Best practices for hybrid networking events

To maximise the impact of hybrid networking events, community leaders should follow a set of best practices:

**Design for hybrid from the outset:** Avoid retrofitting a physical event for virtual audiences at the last minute.

**Prioritise interactivity:** Create moments where both audiences can contribute, not just consume.

**Empower remote participants:** Assign moderators to advocate for virtual attendees during live discussions.

**Simplify access:** Choose platforms and technologies that minimise friction and do not require complex downloads or logins.

**Invest in training:** Ensure speakers, hosts, and technical teams are comfortable engaging both physical and virtual rooms.

**Celebrate contributions equally:** Recognise participation from all attendees, regardless of their mode of access.

These principles ensure that hybrid events become vibrant, inclusive spaces that strengthen rather than fragment community bonds.

## Final thoughts

Hybrid networking events represent the future of community building. They offer a way to bridge the physical and digital worlds, expanding reach without losing depth, and offering flexibility without sacrificing engagement.

By thoughtfully designing experiences that serve both in-person and virtual participants, community builders can create richer, more resilient ecosystems. In doing so, they not only adapt to the changing landscape of how people gather but lead it â setting new standards for connection, collaboration, and collective growth.

## FAQs: Hybrid networking events

### How do you measure the success of a hybrid networking event?

Success can be measured through a combination of engagement metrics, attendee feedback, and business outcomes. Key indicators include attendance rates for both physical and virtual participants, interaction levels during sessions, networking activity, post-event satisfaction surveys, and achievement of specific goals such as lead generation, brand exposure, or community growth.

### What types of communities benefit most from hybrid networking events?

Hybrid networking events are particularly valuable for communities with geographically dispersed members, professional associations, industry-specific groups, educational communities, and global customer bases. Any community that values inclusivity, flexibility, and expanded reach can benefit from a hybrid approach.

### How can you encourage networking between virtual and in-person attendees?

Facilitating cross-format networking requires intentional strategies such as shared chat channels, hybrid breakout sessions, collaborative challenges, and live polls that invite responses from all attendees. Designating virtual ambassadors or creating structured âmeet and mingleâ activities can also bridge the gap between online and onsite participants.

### What are some common mistakes to avoid when planning a hybrid networking event?

Common mistakes include underinvesting in virtual experience quality, treating online attendees as passive viewers, failing to test technology thoroughly, neglecting time zone considerations, and overlooking the need for clear communication about how the hybrid experience will work. Avoiding these pitfalls requires early planning and a participant-first mindset.

### How much lead time is needed to plan a hybrid networking event?

Planning a successful hybrid networking event typically requires a longer timeline than planning a solely physical or virtual event. A lead time of three to six months is ideal, depending on the eventâs size and complexity. This allows time for venue coordination, technology setup, speaker preparation, audience engagement planning, and thorough testing.

# Hybrid communities

Communities that blend online and offline interactions to create a seamless, multi-dimensional member experience.

Communities that blend online and offline interactions to create a seamless, multi-dimensional member experience.

Hybrid communities combine the best of both worldsâonline and offline interactionsâto create rich, multi-dimensional member experiences. As the line between digital and physical spaces continues to blur, hybrid communities are becoming increasingly popular for fostering deeper connections, expanding reach, and accommodating diverse member preferences.

## What are hybrid communities?

Hybrid communities are networks where members engage in both online and offline environments. They leverage digital platforms for virtual interactions while incorporating in-person activities, offering flexibility and inclusivity.

Key characteristics of hybrid communities:

**Dual engagement:** Members can interact through online forums, chats, or events and attend physical meetups or activities.

**Seamless transitions:** The online and offline aspects complement each other, ensuring a cohesive experience.

**Broad accessibility:** Hybrid models cater to members who prefer virtual engagement and those who value face-to-face interaction.

## Why are hybrid communities important?

### Combines flexibility and connection

Members can participate in ways that suit their schedules, locations, and preferences.

### Expands reach

Digital tools allow communities to connect with global audiences, while local events strengthen regional ties.

### Enhances engagement

Hybrid models offer diverse opportunities for members to contribute, collaborate, and build relationships.

### Builds resilience

Communities that operate in both online and offline spaces can adapt to disruptions, such as pandemics or travel restrictions.

### Strengthens bonds

In-person interactions deepen relationships formed online, creating a more cohesive and connected community.

## Key elements of hybrid communities

### Online components

**Community platforms:** Tools like tchopâ¢ facilitate content sharing, discussions, and virtual events.

**Messaging and forums:** Slack, Discord, or WhatsApp enable real-time communication.

**Virtual events:** Webinars, live streams, or virtual conferences keep members engaged across distances.

### Offline components

**Meetups and gatherings:** Local or regional events bring members together in person.

**Workshops and training:** Hands-on experiences build skills and foster collaboration.

**Physical spaces:** Co-working hubs or community centres serve as meeting points for members.

## Benefits of hybrid communities

### Accessibility for all

Hybrid communities accommodate members with varying levels of mobility, availability, or comfort with technology.

### Increased engagement opportunities

Multiple interaction channels keep members active and connected.

### Broader diversity

Combining online and offline participation attracts a wider range of members with different backgrounds and preferences.

### Enhanced member retention

Flexible engagement options reduce the likelihood of members leaving due to logistical challenges or lack of interest.

### Holistic experiences

Members benefit from the immediacy of online interactions and the depth of offline connections.

## How to build and manage hybrid communities

### 1. Define your goals

Identify the purpose of the community and how hybrid interactions will support it. For example:

Strengthen professional networks through virtual discussions and in-person conferences.

Support hobbyists with online tutorials and local workshops.

### 2. Choose the right tools

Select platforms that facilitate both online and offline interactions. Look for features such as event management, discussion boards, and content sharing.

### 3. Plan integrated events

Design events that combine virtual and physical participation. Examples include:

Hybrid conferences with live-streamed and in-person sessions.

Local meetups that recap or extend online discussions.

### 4. Ensure inclusivity

Offer multiple ways to engage so that all members feel welcome, regardless of location or availability.

### 5. Create feedback loops

Regularly gather member input on the effectiveness of online and offline activities to refine your hybrid model.

## Examples of hybrid communities

### Professional associations

Professional groups often host online webinars and discussions alongside in-person networking events or conferences.

### Fitness communities

Hybrid fitness groups combine virtual classes and forums with local meetups for runs, workouts, or yoga sessions.

## Challenges of hybrid communities

### Balancing resources

Managing both online and offline aspects can be resource-intensive. Solution:

Use tools that integrate both components seamlessly.

Prioritise activities that provide the most value to members.

### Maintaining cohesion

Members may feel disconnected if they primarily engage in one format. Solution:

Encourage cross-participation by linking online discussions to offline events.

Share event highlights online to include virtual participants.

### Addressing technical issues

Technical challenges during virtual events can frustrate members. Solution:

Test tools and systems in advance.

Provide support resources or personnel for troubleshooting.

## Measuring success in hybrid communities

To evaluate the effectiveness of hybrid communities, track:

**Engagement metrics:** Participation rates in online forums, virtual events, and in-person activities.

**Retention rates:** Percentage of members who stay active across both formats.

**Feedback scores:** Member satisfaction with online and offline interactions.

**Growth rates:** Increases in membership and event attendance.

**Content interaction:** Views, likes, or comments on shared materials from hybrid events.

## Best practices for hybrid community building

### Align activities with member needs

Understand what your members value in online and offline settings, and design experiences that meet those preferences.

### Leverage technology effectively

Choose platforms that simplify hybrid operations, such as integrating event planning, communication, and analytics.

### Foster cross-format connections

Encourage members who engage online to attend offline events and vice versa. Highlight the benefits of both types of interactions.

### Emphasise inclusivity

Ensure that remote members have access to high-quality content and opportunities to interact, even if they canât attend in person.

### Regularly evaluate and adapt

Use feedback and data to refine your hybrid model, keeping it relevant and engaging.

## Final thoughts

Hybrid communities represent the future of community building, offering flexibility, accessibility, and connection in equal measure. By blending online and offline interactions, they create multi-dimensional experiences that cater to diverse member needs.

## FAQs: Hybrid communities

### What is the main purpose of hybrid communities?

The main purpose of hybrid communities is to combine online and offline interactions to create a flexible, inclusive, and multi-dimensional member experience. This model ensures that members can participate in ways that suit their preferences, schedules, and locations.

### How do hybrid communities differ from purely online or offline communities?

Hybrid communities differ by blending both online and offline elements. Members engage through:

**Online platforms:** Forums, chats, or virtual events.

**Offline activities:** In-person gatherings, workshops, or conferences. This integration allows for a richer and more accessible member experience than either format alone.

### What types of communities benefit most from a hybrid model?

Hybrid communities work well for:

**Professional networks:** Offering virtual webinars and in-person networking events.

**Hobby groups:** Combining online tutorials with physical meetups.

**Non-profits:** Organising online fundraising campaigns alongside local volunteer activities.

**Fitness groups:** Mixing virtual workouts with in-person classes or events.

### How do you manage hybrid events effectively?

To manage hybrid events:

**Plan both formats:** Ensure virtual participants have access to high-quality live streams or recordings.

**Use hybrid-friendly tools:** Platforms like Zoom or tchopâ¢ can integrate online and offline components.

**Encourage interaction:** Create activities that connect in-person and virtual attendees, such as live Q&A or shared challenges.

### How can you keep remote members engaged in a hybrid community?

To engage remote members:

Offer live-streamed or recorded versions of offline events.

Host exclusive virtual activities like webinars or online meetups.

Share highlights and content from in-person events on digital platforms.

Use tools that allow real-time interaction, such as polls or chats.

### What are the challenges of creating hybrid communities?

Challenges include:

**Resource allocation:** Balancing the time and budget needed for both formats.

**Technology issues:** Ensuring reliable tools for virtual interactions.

**Maintaining cohesion:** Preventing a divide between online and offline participants.

### How do hybrid communities handle time zone differences?

To accommodate time zones:

Record events for members unable to join live.

Rotate event times to include different regions.

Use asynchronous engagement tools, such as forums or discussion boards.

### What platforms are ideal for hybrid community building?

Ideal platforms include:

**tchopâ¢:** Combines content sharing, event management, and member engagement.

**Zoom:** For hybrid events with live and virtual attendees.

**Slack or Discord:** Enables communication across online and offline members.

**Eventbrite:** For managing hybrid event registrations.

### How can hybrid communities measure engagement?

To measure engagement:

Track attendance rates for online and offline events.

Monitor activity on digital platforms, such as forum posts or chat participation.

Collect feedback from members about their experiences in both formats.

Analyse retention rates to assess long-term involvement.

### Can hybrid communities work for small groups?

Yes, hybrid communities are highly adaptable for small groups. They:

Allow members to stay connected regardless of location.

Offer affordable tools for virtual engagement.

Provide flexibility in scaling activities as the group grows.

The Ultimate Guide to Hosting Successful Hybrid Events in 2025
==============================================================

[Aniqa Iqbal](https://remo.co/blog-author/aniqa-iqbal)

25 mins

![Image 21: This is a comprehensive guide that goes into details about hybrid events. You will learn about what hybrid events are, different types of hybrid events, steps on how to successfully host one, how to market it, what engagement strategies and best practices to use and much more. ](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/602a043d17d7780d1cce562e/686de26d0b877480b06683ae_hybrid-event.webp)

We used to think of events as places—four walls, a stage, a seat with your name on it. Then, they became links. Calendars turned into login screens.

But somewhere along the way, a new kind of gathering emerged. One that doesn’t make you choose between audiences, but challenges you to rethink how we connect.

[Hybrid events](https://remo.co/blog/what-is-a-hybrid-event) aren’t a compromise between formats; they’re a reimagining of presence, participation, and possibility. They ask: What if every experience could be designed for both immediacy and accessibility? What if engagement wasn’t bound by time zones, travel, or tech limitations? Hybrid events combine the energy and intimacy of in-person experiences with the reach and flexibility of virtual event participation. But they’re not just about logistics. At their core, hybrid events embody a new philosophy that prioritizes accessibility, personalization, and meaningful interaction in every moment.

In this guide, we’ll explore the various types of hybrid events, discuss why they matter more than ever, and provide insights on how to create one that not only meets expectations but also truly resonates with every attendee, wherever they are.

Key Takeaways
-------------

Hybrid events are a strategy. They’re designed to unify your audience across virtual and in-person formats.

Engagement must be intentional. Hybrid success depends on creating moments that connect both audiences meaningfully.

Technology is your enabler. The right platform provides seamless networking, real-time content access, and interactive tools that work for everyone.

Planning for both audiences is non-negotiable. Every detail hould be tailored to support both formats equally. 

Types of Hybrid Events
----------------------

![Image 22: A hybrid event taking place between office team and remote team joining virtually. ](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/602a043d17d7780d1cce562e/6866596bcd8a4396349012ac_hybrid%20marketing%20(1)%202.webp)

Hybrid events adapt to who you’re bringing together and why. Internal hybrid events keep teams aligned across locations, making meetings, training, and company-wide kickoffs more accessible. On the other hand, external hybrid events expand your reach, turning conferences, trade shows, and product launches into global experiences. Moreover, transitioning to a hybrid format isn’t just about scale. It’s a more sustainable, cost-effective approach that reduces travel, lowers expenses, and minimizes your carbon footprint.

Here are the [different types of hybrid events](https://remo.co/blog/10-extraordinary-hybrid-event-examples-that-will-inspire-you-in-2021):

**Internal Hybrid Events**

![Image 23: A small internal hybrid event](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/602a043d17d7780d1cce562e/686659b20b09c02a262c4d25_internal.webp)

Internal hybrid events bridge the gap between in-office and remote teams, creating an inclusive experience where no one feels like an afterthought. Hybrid formats blend real-time, face-to-face interactions with virtual accessibility, ensuring strengthened company culture, ideas flowing freely, engagement staying high, and no one missing a beat, regardless of location or time zone. They're less about routine meetings and more about energizing alignment moments like sales kickoffs, global town halls, company sprints, and internal product launches. Hybrid formats ensure culture, communication, and collaboration remain strong across time zones and job functions.

**External Hybrid Events**

![Image 24: A large-scale external hybrid event.](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/602a043d17d7780d1cce562e/686659ca07dee556bb9a54c1_external.webp)

External hybrid events extend your reach beyond physical boundaries, welcoming clients, prospects, industry leaders, and even the broader public into the conversation. These events are not just for your organization and team members but for everyone. From [hybrid conferences](https://remo.co/blog/hybrid-conference-events) and [trade shows](https://remo.co/guides/virtual-trade-show) to product launches, seminars, and live entertainment, these events blend the energy of in-person with the accessibility of digital. They maximize attendance, build more profound engagement, and amplify your brand’s presence, turning conferences, trade shows, and networking events into global experiences.

Hybrid events are a way to create more inclusive, dynamic experiences. Whether you’re uniting teams across time zones or engaging a global audience, the right approach makes all the difference. It’s not about choosing one over the other but about making every interaction count.

Key Differences Between Hybrid, Virtual, and In-Person Events
-------------------------------------------------------------

![Image 25: An image clearly depicting how attendees take part in each type of event format.](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/602a043d17d7780d1cce562e/686659eb07dee556bb9a6688_diffs.webp)

3 Formats. 3 Choices. 3 Different Worlds. Imagine standing at a crossroads. One path leads to a buzzing convention center, filled with handshakes, conversations, and the electric hum of a live audience. Another path takes you to a sleek digital space, where attendees log in from all over the world, joining breakout rooms and swapping emojis. And the third? A blend of both; a foot in the physical world, a foot in the virtual, balancing real-time energy with digital convenience.

Each format offers a unique experience. Which one to go for depends on what kind of experience you want to create. Do you want global accessibility or the intimacy of in-person connection? Are you aiming for streamlined efficiency or a dynamic, multi-layered experience? In this section we’ll cover the key [differences between virtual, hybrid](https://remo.co/blog/virtual-vs-hybrid-events), and in-person events:.

**Definition and Structure**

*   **In-Person Events:** The classic format. Physical venues, fixed schedules, and all the on-ground logistics that come with them. Attendees need to be physically present, meaning planning includes everything from travel and accommodation to catering and seating charts.
*   **Virtual Events:**These are fully online, with no flights, hotels, or venues. Whether it’s a live-streamed keynote, an interactive webinar, or a pre-recorded session, everything happens on a screen. Attendees can tune in from anywhere, making them the most accessible and hassle-free event format.
*   **Hybrid Events:** A mix of online and in-person experiences. Some attendees are physically present, while others join virtually. This can be as simple as a livestreamed conference or as immersive as a fully interactive event where virtual attendees can network, ask questions, and participate just like those on-site.

‍**Audience Reach and Accessibility**

*   **In-Person Events:** Exclusive, but with limits. If you’re not there, you’re out of luck. Venue capacity, travel constraints, and scheduling conflicts can keep potential attendees away. Great for in-person connections and hands-on experiences, but not ideal for those who can’t make the trip.
*   **Virtual Events:**Borders? What borders? Virtual events erase geographic limits, anyone with Wi-Fi can attend. Just grab a laptop (or even a phone), log in, and boom, you’re in. Whether you're in New York or New Delhi, everyone gets a front-row seat.
*   **Hybrid Events:**Maximum flexibility – attendees get to choose what works best for them; join in person or tune in from the comfort of their couch. Whether they’re halfway across the world or just avoiding rush-hour traffic, hybrid events present flexibility at its finest.

**Engagement and Networking Opportunities**

*   **In-Person Events:**Nothing beats the energy of face-to-face interactions. Spontaneous conversations, impromptu meetups, and real-time product demos make networking effortless. You shake hands, exchange business cards, and build relationships the old-school way, with no buffering, no lag, just physical human connection.
*   **Virtual Events:** Digital-first, but still packed with opportunities to connect, just in different ways. Attendees can engage through live chats, Q&As, virtual networking lounges, and polls. While there’s no physical water cooler conversations, there’s still plenty of room to connect, chat, and meet new people for better [virtual event engagement](https://remo.co/blog/virtual-event-engagement).
*   **Hybrid Events:** Engagement here is a sliding scale. At its simplest, a hybrid event is just a live stream. Virtual attendees watch but don’t participate. But with the right virtual tech, like Remo and its various interactive features, the online experience at a hybrid event can be just as interactive as the in-person one.

**Cost Considerations**

*   **In-Person Events:**A pricier option than virtual events when you factor in venue rental, travel, accommodation, catering, staffing, and AV setup. For large-scale gatherings, costs can quickly add up as you provide everything from physical signage to meals, seating, lighting, and on-site technical support.
*   **Virtual Events:**The budget-friendly winner. No venue, no catering, no travel expenses, just a digital platform and a solid plan. If cost efficiency is a priority, this is the way to go. Read more on [virtual event pricing](https://remo.co/blog/virtual-event-pricing).
*   **Hybrid Events:** A double investment. You’re paying for the full in-person experience and the tech to stream, engage, and connect virtual attendees. That means costs for venues, travel, and digital tools, making this format potentially the most expensive. The upside? A larger audience reach can justify the extra spend.

**Flexibility and Planning**

*   **In-Person Events:**The most demanding in terms of logistics. From securing a venue to managing travel, catering, seating, and scheduling, every detail matters. These events take the longest to plan, with little room for last-minute changes.
*   **Virtual Events:**The easiest to pull off. No venue logistics, no catering concerns. Just set up the platform, schedule your sessions, and go live. [Virtual event planning](https://remo.co/blog/the-ultimate-guide-to-virtual-event-planning) timelines are shorter, and last-minute changes are much easier to handle.
*   **Hybrid Events:**More complex, more moving parts. You’re essentially running two events in one, coordinating an in-person experience while ensuring the virtual side runs smoothly. From managing livestreams to engaging both audiences equally, hybrid events require detailed planning and strong execution, but this guide has everything you need to get started.

## Hybrid networks address the weak points that currently limit federal networks -- a lack of bandwidth, budget, capacity and availability, particularly at agency field offices.

Under the Cross-Agency Prioritization goals set by the current administration, the federal government has made significant progress in modernizing outdated legacy technologies. Initiatives like the Federal Cloud Computing Strategy and the Federal IT Acquisition Reform Act have enabled easier access to more capable technologies, but they have also put a heavier burden on federal networks. Current technology trends, which range from the connected devices of the Internet of Things to bring-your-own-device initiatives and high-performance applications, are demanding more and more bandwidth. Unfortunately, the one aspect of federal technology modernization that has been overlooked is also the most important: the network backbone on which all the new technologies rely to operate.

Although the concern over inadequate bandwidth is widespread among federal IT professionals, these pressing needs are not reflected in agency budgets. Too often, agencies do not have the funding they think is needed to increase their bandwidth to levels that would support optimized and increasingly automated day-to-day operations. This situation leads to patchwork solutions that are limited in scalability and highly inefficient, both technologically and financially. What is needed is a new federal network standard.

Thankfully a model for such a standard is available, and already being adopted by private sector enterprises. Hybrid networks -- systems that blend traditional Multiprotocol Label Switching (MLPS) networks with managed broadband -- address the weak points that currently limit federal networks: a lack of bandwidth, budget, capacity and availability, particularly at the agency’s field offices.

Hybrid networks provide additional bandwidth by either supplementing or replacing the traditional dedicated access circuits of MPLS networks with the bandwidth of a managed broadband network. For agency networks that must support a growing number of connected devices and new applications, especially at distributed locations, hybrid networks prevent congestion when the traditional network access technologies cannot handle the volume of higher traffic on their own. The hybrid model achieves this by taking advantage of the Internet and its lower price points and by intelligently prioritizing and routing traffic when needed. A managed hybrid network can help add bandwidth and alleviate the strain on network capacity.

Currently, when a federal agency needs additional bandwidth at distributed sites, it will purchase additional (sometimes multiple) T1 lines that are expensive for the limited amount of capacity they offer. The hybrid network alternative-- implementing managed broadband at these sites -- can offer higher levels of bandwidth at a substantially lower price.

The hybrid network solution can also add desperately needed redundancy and resiliency to federal networks. Currently many agencies consider their networks to have built-in resiliency because they have two different terrestrial lines acquired from two separate service providers, whether fiber, cable or DSL. However, what agencies may not be considering is that these lines, although provided by two different companies delivering adequate bandwidth, are often routed through the same underground conduits. This approach yields limited or no additional gain in network availability should a natural disaster, deliberate attack or human error sever one of those “side-by-side” conduits. Indeed, only implementing a true alternate communications path, which broadband satellite provides, increases the overall availability. Should the primary line be disrupted or lost, all mission-critical traffic could be seamlessly shifted to a satellite link during the terrestrial network outage.

Despite the tangible improvements that a hybrid network solution offers, some agencies will still be hesitant to adopt it because of security concerns related to Internet-based transport as opposed to dedicated networks -- concerns that Rep. William Hurd (R-Texas), chairman of the IT Subcommittee of the House Committee on Government Oversight and Reform, recently described as a “huge misconception.” This is because cloud-based technologies are capable of utilizing the highest level of security standards, such as the AES-256 encryption standard used by intelligence agencies to protect classified information. Completely managed, secure end-to-end solutions are able to offer this kind of encryption to protect sensitive information, both at rest and in ßtransit.

With rising bandwidth demands and plateauing budgets, the situation is clear: Agencies must adopt the new, cheaper and more efficient standard -- hybrid networks. Every day that they perpetuate the antiquated model of legacy networks is one more day of wasted budget dollars and underperforming networks, limiting the potential of new applications and cloud-based solutions.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Hybrid events merge in-person and virtual experiences, facilitating diverse networking opportunities that enhance attendee engagement and broaden participation across geographical boundaries.
Utilizing advanced platforms and tools enables seamless connections between remote and local attendees, ensuring access to valuable content and networking opportunities for all participants.
Hybrid formats foster interactions among diverse communities, promoting collaboration and knowledge sharing that transcends traditional barriers, enriching the overall event experience.
